name: Rapa and cMRI older-adults registry
summary: Completed open-label phase 2 single-group substudy of 1 mg daily oral rapamycin for eight weeks in adults aged 70 to 95, using cardiac MRI measures of systolic function, diastolic function, aortic cross-sectional area, and aortic distensibility.
population: Adults aged 70 to 95 years in good general health with stable chronic conditions allowed

trial_details.expected_results_window: The registry lists actual primary and study completion on September 9, 2025 and had no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
trial_details.horizon_note: Completed no-results older-adult cardiac-function watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: This completed registry directly tests low-dose rapamycin in older adults with functional cardiac MRI endpoints, so absent results are important limiting coverage context.
